title: How we contain Claude across products
claims: Claude Code users approved roughly 93% of permission prompts in Anthropic telemetry.
Containment, filesystem boundaries, and egress controls limit blast radius beyond prompt-level supervision.

title: How we built Claude Code auto mode: a safer way to skip permissions
claims: High approval rates create approval fatigue.
Broad arbitrary-code permissions are excluded from automated approval.

title: Making Claude Code more secure and autonomous with sandboxing
claims: Filesystem and network isolation reduce reliance on repeated permission prompts.
Credentials can be injected by a trusted proxy rather than exposed to the agent.

title: Task-Completion Time Horizons of Frontier AI Models
claims: Time horizon is the human-expert task duration at which an agent is predicted to succeed at a stated reliability.
The metric describes a benchmark task suite and is not the wall-clock time an agent can autonomously operate.

title: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications for 2026
claims: Persistent context poisoning and excessive agency are first-class agentic application risks.
The list is versioned for 2026 and must be rechecked when OWASP updates it.

title: Combinatorial Methods for Trust and Assurance
claims: In the studied systems, most failures involved one or two factors and progressively fewer involved more factors.
Four-way to six-way coverage provided strong assurance in multiple studies but is not a universal guarantee.

title: SWE-Skills-Bench: Do Agent Skills Actually Help in Real-World Software Engineering?
claims: The benchmark pairs public skills with pinned repositories and executable acceptance tests.
Its reported results show many skills did not improve pass rate and some regressed, so skill value must be measured rather than assumed.

title: Agent Skill Evaluation and Evolution: Frameworks and Benchmarks
claims: Skill evaluation should cover utility, safety, generalization, efficiency, and evolution rather than task adherence alone.

title: SkillGrad: Optimizing Agent Skills Like Gradient Descent
claims: Recurring diagnostics and contrastive evidence can stabilize skill updates better than one-off reflection.

title: OPID: On-Policy Skill Distillation for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
claims: Skill distillation from trajectories is sensitive to the policy and state distribution that produced those trajectories.

title: Statistical Concepts - Probabilistic Data - Forecast User Guide
claims: Brier Skill Score compares forecast Brier score against a reference such as climatology.
A raw Brier score must be interpreted relative to event base rate when comparing heterogeneous events.

title: AGENTS.md
claims: AGENTS.md is a cross-tool convention, but each tool's discovery and precedence behavior must be verified.

title: The /llms.txt file
claims: llms.txt is a proposal and should be adopted only when target consumers and maintenance value are identified.
